architecture_plan.title: Human-level Reward Design via Coding Large Language Models - Demo Implementation Architecture
architecture_plan.overview: This architecture demonstrates a modular implementation of a method that designs human-level rewards using large language models (LLMs) through coding. It integrates environment context modeling, evolutionary search for reward function optimization, and reward reflection mechanisms to iteratively improve reward functions. The system supports both human initialization and feedback-driven refinement of reward functions.

modules.purpose: Models the environment as context for reward design, capturing state and action spaces to inform reward function generation.
Performs evolutionary search over reward function candidates to optimize reward design based on performance metrics.
Implements reward reflection mechanisms to refine reward functions using LLMs, incorporating feedback from human evaluators.
Provides an interface for collecting and integrating human feedback into the reward design process.
Generates and manages prompts for LLMs to produce reward functions, supporting both initialization and reflection phases.
Evaluates reward function performance using simulated environments and metrics to guide evolutionary search.
Manages system configuration, including environment details, search parameters, and feedback integration settings.

framework_suggestions: PyTorch or TensorFlow for deep learning components
Docker for containerization of LLM services
FastAPI or Flask for RESTful API interfaces
Ray or Optuna for hyperparameter tuning and evolutionary search
LangChain or LlamaIndex for LLM prompt engineering and integration
